sourceMovie.title: Mandy
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Mandy for the same reason it broke through in 2018: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a action rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Panos Cosmatos keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The fantasy and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Nosferatu
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Mandy clicked for you, Nosferatu (2024) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Mandy?
faq.answer: Try Nosferatu, Wes Craven's New Nightmare, Get Out—each shares DNA with Mandy while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Mandy?
faq.answer: Mandy (2018) is best described as Fantasy, Action, Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Mandy worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this fantasy lane.
